results.description:
Location: On-site at Picatinny Arsenal, Wharton, NJ
Clearance Required: U.S. Citizen / Public Trust
Employment Type: Full-Time

We're looking for dynamic Administrative Support Specialists to join our team at Picatinny Arsenal, supporting the U.S. Army. If you're a motivated self-starter with a passion for helping customers tackle complex challenges, this is the opportunity for you! Do you thrive on learning new tools, processes, and technologies that can make a real impact? At Ghostwerks, you'll be a valued team member where your growth and professional development matter. Join us, and let's drive change together while advancing your career!

Ghostwerks is a Service Disabled Veteran Owned Small Business (SDVOSB) that specializes in navigating the intricate landscape of the defense and civilian government space. Ghostwerks is committed to providing the highest quality support and services to our customers. We offer a wide range of services, including Management Consulting, Mission Readiness, Technology Solutions, Acquisition Support, and Government Contractor Support. Our services are designed to help organizations of all sizes achieve their goals and reach their full potential.

POSITION DESCRIPTION:

The Administrative Support Specialist provides administrative support specifically dedicated to the requirements of the project team. They may assist in budget preparation and financial control activities. Assisting in the preparation of presentations and control of records, statistics, and reports regarding operations, financial tracking, and personnel changes using various databases and programs. Assisting in the development of reports and presentations using software packages for word processing, spreadsheets, database, desktop publishing and graphics (MS Office Suite). Composes, prepares, edits and proofreads letters, reports and other correspondence. Administers client- specific programs, projects, and/or processes. Serves as liaison regarding administrative issues related to purchasing, personnel, facilities, and operations. Coordinates meetings and appointments between program manager, client, and external-parties. Support may also include making travel arrangements, meeting/event arrangements and preparations. Resolve questions and problems and escalate issues to higher levels. In general, work complexity and responsibility will be greater at higher levels.

RESPONSIBILITIES:

  • Develop spreadsheet to track the progress of the quality audit across tank ammo production
  • Develop and distribute schedules (Microsoft Project) for production acceptance reviews, tests, audits.
  • Coordinate with multiple contractors and subcontractors to ascertain and verify timeframes of production runs and changes.
  • Create master schedule with all programs from tank ammo (training rounds, service rounds,), and distribute to AC and PM team members.
  • Create power point slides of schedules for briefings and presentations.
  • Coordinate travelers schedules to cover all tests, production facilities of interest. Maintain a list/spreadsheet of all travelers.
  • Identify scheduling conflicts, and deconflict travel schedules and activities of all parties.
  • Coordinate travel itineraries, hotel, arrangements as required. Support voucher review.
  • Conduct weekly meetings to coordinate activities amongst lead members to insure production coverage. Power point presentation required.
  • Obtain directions to facilities for travelers, and identify area restaurants, and hotels for traveler convenience.
  • Coordinate JPAS, security of all travelers.
  • Maintain a list of contractor facility POCs and phone numbers.
  • Collect trip reports from travelers Identifying problems areas concerns.

REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

  • Positive and enthusiastic attitude
  • Strong desire to learn, drive work, and self-initiative
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills
  • Microsoft Project experience
  • MS Office PowerPoint experience
  • SharePoint experience
  • High School Diploma
  • Must be a U.S. Citizen
  • Must be able to successfully obtain a Public Trust

ADDITIONAL JOB INFORMATION

COMPETENCIES

  • Proficiency with word processing, spreadsheet, project planning and presentation software
  • Ability to quickly learn and process information
  • Organization and time management skills

WORK ENVIRONMENT AND PHYSICAL DEMANDS

The work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee encounters while performing the essential functions of the job. Reasonable accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

  • Customer Location: Picatinny Arsenal (Wharton), New Jersey
  • Type of environment: Office with some telework
  • Noise level: Low Normal Office Environment
  • Work schedule: Schedule is customer core business working hours.
  • Amount of Travel: Local Travel

PHYSICAL DEMANDS

The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to use hands to handle, feel, touch; reach with hands and arms; talk and hear. The vision requirements include close vision, distance vision, peripheral vision, depth perception, and ability to adjust focus.

EQUAL EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITY

To provide equal employment and advancement opportunities to all individuals, employment decisions will be based on merit, qualifications, and abilities. Ghostwerks does not discriminate against any person because of race, color, creed, religion, sex, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected by law (referred to as "protected status"). This nondiscrimination policy extends to all terms, conditions, and privileges of employment as well as the use of all company facilities, participation in all company-sponsored activities, and all employment actions such as promotions, compensation, benefits, and termination of employment.

Ghostwerks is committed to ensuring that our online application process provides an equal employment opportunity to all job seekers, including individuals with disabilities. If you believe you need reasonable accommodation in order to search for a job opening or to submit an application, please contact info@ghostwerksllc.com

for assistance.

The Business Analyst III serves as a primary execution-level analyst responsible for detailed compliance reviews, financial analysis, and stakeholder coordination across multiple concession workstreams. This mid-level role ensures thorough validation of submissions against key Port Authority policies, including the Street Pricing Policy (+15%) and Value for Money (VFM) requirements, while supporting the overall integrity of the concessions program across approximately 800 locations. The BA III operates with significant independence, documents findings, and interfaces directly with stakeholders to drive timely resolutions and maintain program standards.

Essential Functions

  • Conduct detailed reviews and validations of Full Market Basket (FMB) submissions from concessionaires at approximately 800 locations.
  • Evaluate compliance with critical policies such as the Street Pricing Policy (maximum +15% variance) and Value for Money (VFM) standards.
  • Perform comprehensive comparative pricing analyses utilizing data from 3–6 benchmark locations or sources.
  • Document detailed findings, discrepancies, root causes, and recommended corrective actions.
  • Serve as a key point of contact, interfacing directly with Terminal Operators (TOs) and Concession Management Companies (CMCs) to address compliance matters.
  • Maintain and update the FMB Tracker and associated support systems with accurate, timely data.
  • Prepare weekly status updates, monthly reporting inputs, non-compliance summaries, and other deliverables while ensuring all reviews meet strict timelines (typically within 30 days).

Required Education and Experience

  • MUST BE UNITED STATES CITIZEN 
  • 5–10 years of experience in financial analysis, consulting, program support, compliance, or audit-related roles.
  • Bachelor's degree in Business, Finance, Accounting, or a related field required.

Competencies

  • Advanced financial and analytical capabilities with strong attention to detail.
  • Prior experience in compliance review, audit environments, or regulatory programs.
  • Proven ability to manage multiple concurrent workstreams and meet tight deadlines.
  • Excellent stakeholder coordination and interpersonal communication skills.
  • Proficiency in synthesizing data into clear, actionable documentation and reports.

Engineer Technician Job Description

  • Must be a U.S. Citizen
  • Must be able to successfully obtain a Security Clearance

We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced Mechanical Technician. The Electronics & Mechanical Assembly Technician will build, troubleshoot, rework, and test electro-mechanical assemblies to defined workmanship standards. This role requires excellent attention to detail, strong process discipline, and the ability to follow work instructions, drawings, and inspection criteria. The technician will also support inventory traceability, travelers, and quality documentation

This position requires a motivated self-starter who can effectively learn new tools, processes, and technologies. At Ghostwerks, team members are valued, and their growth and professional development are important.

Ghostwerks is a Service-Disabled Veteran Owned Small Business (SDVOSB) that specializes in navigating the intricate landscape of the defense and civilian government space. Ghostwerks is committed to providing the highest quality support and services to our customers. We offer a wide range of services, including Solution Engineering, Mission Readiness, Technology Solutions, Engineering Support, and Government Contractor Support. Our services are designed to help organizations of all sizes achieve their goals and reach their full potential.

Essential Duties and Responsibilities

  • Perform electronics assembly including hand soldering and rework (SMT and through-hole as applicable) to defined soldering/workmanship specifications (e.g., IPC-style requirements)
  • Execute cable/harness fabrication: cutting, stripping, crimping, terminating, continuity checks, and labeling per drawings
  • Assemble and integrate electro-mechanical subassemblies including fasteners, adhesives, torque application, alignment, and fit checks
  • Conduct in-process and final functional checks using basic test equipment (DMM, power supplies, microscopes, continuity testers, etc.)
  • Perform inspection support: verify build to drawings, measure critical features, document results, and support nonconformance (NCM) disposition processes
  • Maintain traceability for parts and builds (lot/serial tracking, travelers, barcodes/labels, and ERP or spreadsheet-based logging as required)
  • Follow ESD, cleanliness, and tool control requirements; maintain a tidy and organized workstation
  • Provide feedback for continuous improvement help refine work instructions, tool lists, and test steps based on build experience
  • Support packaging, kitting, and staging for production builds and engineering iterations

Qualifications (Required)

Work Status:

  • Must be a U.S. Citizen
  • Must be able to successfully obtain a Security Clearance

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience with conformal coating, epoxy/potting, adhesives, and controlled cure processes
  • Familiarity with ESD programs and electronics handling best practices
  • Experience with ERP/MRP systems, barcode labeling, kitting, and inventory control
  • Basic troubleshooting skills: identifying root cause for build/test failures and executing corrective rework steps
  • Prior experience in regulated or high-reliability environments (aerospace, defense, medical, industrial)

Certifications (Preferred):

IPC training/certification (e.g., IPC-A-610 and/or J-STD-001) or equivalent workmanship background

Additional Job Information

Competencies

  • Ability to quickly learn and process information
  • Organization and time management skills

Work Environment and Physical Demands

The work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee encounters while performing the essential functions of the job. Reasonable accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

  • Customer Location: On-Site – Picatinny Arsenal Wharton, NJ
  • Type of environment: Manufacturing
  • Noise level: Mid-High – Normal Manufacturing Environment
  • Work schedule: Schedule is customer core business working hours, typically between 7am-5pm.
  • Amount of Travel: Less than 5%

Physical Demands

The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to use hands to handle, feel, touch; reach with hands and arms; talk and hear. The vision requirements include close vision, distance vision, peripheral vision, depth perception, and ability to adjust focus.

Equal Employment Opportunity

To provide equal employment and advancement opportunities to all individuals, employment decisions will be based on merit, qualifications, and abilities. Ghostwerks does not discriminate against any person because of race, color, creed, religion, sex, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected by law (referred to as "protected status"). This nondiscrimination policy extends to all terms, conditions, and privileges of employment as well as the use of all company facilities, participation in all company-sponsored activities, and all employment actions such as promotions, compensation, benefits, and termination of employment.

Ghostwerks is committed to ensuring that our online application process provides an equal employment opportunity to all job seekers, including individuals with disabilities. If you believe you need reasonable accommodation in order to search for a job opening or to submit an application, please contact info@ghostwerksllc.com for assistance.

Why Join Ghostwerks LLC?

  • Work with a growing SDVOSB company in the government contracting space.
  • Engage in meaningful work supporting federal contracts and mission-critical operations.
  • Opportunity for professional growth and leadership in a dynamic environment.
  • Competitive salary and benefits package to include; insurance (health, dental, life, disability), and 401k with company match.

If you are a results-driven professional with experience in welding in a manufacturing setting, we encourage you to apply and contribute to Ghostwerks LLCs continued success.

Mechanical Engineering Job Description

We are seeking a highly experienced (Mechanical Engineering) to provide direct engineering and technical support to the Development Command Armaments Center (DEVCOM AC), Fuze and Precision Armaments Directorate (FPAD), Maneuver Support Division (MSD). This role supports the full life-cycle engineering mission for legacy and future armament systems, including research, development, modernization, production, fielding, and sustainment activities at Picatinny Arsenal and associated project locations.

This position requires a Mid to senior-level engineer and trusted technical advisor who can independently analyze complex engineering challenges, support Integrated Product Teams (IPTs), and represent engineering interests during technical reviews and customer engagements. At Ghostwerks, team members are valued for their expertise, professionalism, and commitment to mission success.

Ghostwerks is a Service Disabled Veteran Owned Small Business (SDVOSB) specializing in engineering support, mission readiness, and government contractor support across the defense sector. We are committed to delivering high-quality, responsive support to our government customers.

Essential Duties and Responsibilities

Armaments Modernization & Production Engineering

  • Provide expert mechanical engineering solutions to resolve technical challenges related to armaments, ammunition, and maneuver support systems across research, development, modernization, and production phases.
  • Apply in-depth knowledge of life-cycle management for armament and ammunition systems, from concept development through sustainment.
  • Support engineering efforts for legacy systems currently fielded as well as emerging capabilities aligned with Army 2030 and Army 2040 initiatives.

Systems Engineering & Integrated Product Team (IPT) Support

  • Provide engineering support during key technical, design, and program reviews, serving as a subject matter expert for mechanical systems and integration concerns.
  • Participate in IPTs and working groups with government customers and stakeholders to identify risks, resolve engineering issues, and recommend technically sound solutions.
  • Support the development of procurement packages as new contract requirements emerge.
  • Conduct technical evaluations of contractor proposals and support source selection activities as required.
  • Provide engineering support for Engineering Change Proposals (ECPs) and Requests for Variance (RFVs).
  • Support failure analysis activities during First Article Testing (FAT) and Lot Acceptance Testing (LAT).
  • Review and provide technical feedback on contract deliverables and responses to customer Requests for Information (RFIs).

Technical Documentation, Reporting, and Communication

  • Draft clear, concise technical documentation reflecting program status, risks, engineering concerns, and recommendations.
  • Develop formal technical memorandums, engineering reports, and briefing materials for government stakeholders.
  • Communicate complex engineering concepts effectively to both technical and non-technical audiences.

Qualifications (Required)

Work Status:

  • Must be a U.S. Citizen
  • Must be able to successfully obtain a Security Clearance

Education:

  • Bachelor's degree in mechanical engineering or systems engineering or a closely related engineering discipline

Experience:

  • Minimum of 2-7 years of progressively responsible mechanical engineering experience
  • Demonstrated experience supporting DoD or Army armament, ammunition, or maneuver support programs
  • Experience providing senior-level engineering analysis, technical reviews, and customer-facing support

Skills:

  • Strong understanding of systems engineering principles and life-cycle engineering
  • Ability to interpret and evaluate technical drawings, specifications, and military standards
  • Experience with engineering change management and technical evaluation of contractor deliverables
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills

Qualifications (Preferred):

  • Prior experience supporting DEVCOM, Picatinny Arsenal, or similar DoD R&D organizations
  • Familiarity with Army acquisition processes, test and evaluation activities, and production readiness reviews
  • Experience working within Integrated Product Teams (IPTs)

Additional Job Information

Competencies

  • Ability to quickly learn and process information
  • Organization and time management skills

Work Environment and Physical Demands

The work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee encounters while performing the essential functions of the job. Reasonable accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

  • Customer Location: On-site at Picatinny Arsenal, NJ, and other project-related locations
  • Type of Environment: Office, laboratory, and manufacturing/test environments
  • Work Schedule: Customer core business hours
  • Travel: As required to support program activities

Physical Demands

The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to use hands to handle, feel, touch; reach with hands and arms; talk and hear. The vision requirements include close vision, distance vision, peripheral vision, depth perception, and ability to adjust focus.

Equal Employment Opportunity

To provide equal employment and advancement opportunities to all individuals, employment decisions will be based on merit, qualifications, and abilities. Ghostwerks does not discriminate against any person because of race, color, creed, religion, sex, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected by law (referred to as "protected status"). This nondiscrimination policy extends to all terms, conditions, and privileges of employment as well as the use of all company facilities, participation in all company-sponsored activities, and all employment actions such as promotions, compensation, benefits, and termination of employment.

Ghostwerks is committed to ensuring that our online application process provides an equal employment opportunity to all job seekers, including individuals with disabilities. If you believe you need reasonable accommodation in order to search for a job opening or to submit an application, please contact info@ghostwerksllc.com for assistance.

Why Join Ghostwerks LLC?

  • Support mission-critical Army and DoD engineering programs
  • Work with a growing SDVOSB company in the government contracting space.
  • Engage in meaningful work supporting federal contracts and mission-critical operations.
  • Opportunity for professional growth and leadership in a dynamic environment.
  • Competitive salary and benefits package to include; insurance (health, dental, life, disability), and 401k with company match.

If you are a results-driven professional with experience in Armaments Engineering & Modernization, we encourage you to apply and contribute to Ghostwerks LLCs continued success

Project Support Specialist:

Ghostwerks is seeking an organized and driven Project Support Specialist to assist project teams with coordination, scheduling, and administrative support tasks. This role is ideal for someone who thrives in a fast-paced environment, is highly detail-oriented, and enjoys working collaboratively across teams.

The Project Support Specialist will help track project progress, maintain project documentation, coordinate schedules, and support overall project execution to ensure tasks are completed on time and efficiently.

Ghostwerks is a Service-Disabled Veteran Owned Small Business (SDVOSB) that specializes in navigating the intricate landscape of the defense and civilian government space. Ghostwerks is committed to providing the highest quality support and services to our customers. We offer a wide range of services, including Solution Engineering, Mission Readiness, Technology Solutions, Engineering Support, and Government Contractor Support. Our services are designed to help organizations of all sizes achieve their goals and reach their full potential.

Essential Duties and Responsibilities

· Assist in coordinating project tasks and ensuring deadlines are met

· Track project progress and communicate issues or delays to the Project Manager

· Prepare and maintain project documentation, including status reports, meeting minutes, and project files

· Maintain and update project schedules, databases, and project management tools

· Provide administrative support to project teams, including scheduling meetings and coordinating communications

· Assist with development and maintenance of project plans and timelines

· Participate in project meetings and provide support as needed

· Track action items and follow up on outstanding tasks to ensure timely completion

· Coordinate communication between internal teams, customers, subcontractors, and stakeholders

· Prepare presentations, reports, and briefing materials for leadership reviews

· Assist with data collection, reporting metrics, and project performance tracking

· Maintain document control and ensure project documentation complies with company and customer requirements

· Support contract deliverables and compliance tracking

· Assist with risk management activities and issue resolution efforts

· Help identify process improvements to increase project efficiency and organization

· Support scheduling, resource coordination, and logistics across multiple projects

· Maintain sensitive project information with professionalism and discretion

· Collaborate with internal teams to ensure project deliverables are completed efficiently

Qualifications (Required)

Work Status:

  • Must be a U.S. Citizen
  • Must be able to successfully obtain a Security Clearance

Preferred Qualifications

· Background in Government contracting preferred

· Proven experience as a Project Coordinator, Project Support Specialist, or similar role

· Experience using project management software such as MS Project

· Strong organizational and multitasking abilities

· Excellent verbal and written communication skills

· Ability to work effectively in a team-oriented environment

· Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite, especially Excel and PowerPoint

· Strong attention to detail and problem-solving skills

· Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment

Skills:

· Attention to detail

· Written and verbal communication

· Multi-tasking in fast-paced environments

· Problem-solving and critical thinking

· Team collaboration and interpersonal skills

· Scheduling and calendar management

· Meeting coordination and minute-taking

· Project documentation and record keeping

· Risk identification and issue tracking

· Data entry and reporting accuracy

· Process improvement and workflow organization

· Ability to prioritize competing deadlines

· Administrative and operational support

· Resource coordination and logistics support

· Confidentiality and professionalism
